title: Bridge Essay: Shivering and nonshivering thermogenesis in skeletal muscles.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Heat/Fire** (Phase 3) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Heat/Fire to mechanistic neuroscience through Shivering, and, nonshivering, thermogenesis, in.

**Shivering and nonshivering thermogenesis in skeletal muscles.** — Handb Clin Neurol. PMID: 30454588. DOI: 10.1016/B978-0-444-63912-7.00010-2. Humans have inherited complex neural circuits which drive behavioral, somatic, and autonomic thermoregulatory responses to defend their body temperature. While they are well adapted to dissipate heat in warm climates, they have areduced capacity to preserve it in cold environments. Consequently, heat production is critical to defending their core temperature. As in other large mammals, skeletal muscles are the primary source of heat production recruited in cold-exposed humans.
